title: Latest news about the Chemical Management Plan - Canada.ca
content:
## Latest news about the Chemicals Management Plan
- Environment Canada Media Room
- Health Canada Media Room
- Ketones Group : The Final Assessment for the Ketones Group was published. The Risk Management Approach for Ketones, specifically: 2-Butanone (Methyl Ethyl Ketone; MEK) and 4-Methyl-2-pentanone (Methyl Isobutyl Ketone; MIBK) was also published for a 60-day public  comment period ending on July 15, 2026. In addition, 2,4-PD is proposed for addition to the Watch List. [2026-05-16]
- Federal Environmental Quality Guidelines : The federal environmental quality  guidelines for Rare earth elements were published. [2026-05-15]
- Request to assess a substance under the Canadian Environmental Protection Act, 1999 : A decision on a request to assess Desflurane was published. [2026-05-01]
- The Watch List : The final Watch List Approach was published. [2026-04-28]
- Alkyl Halides Group : The Final Assessment for the Alkyl Halides Group was published. The Risk Management Approach for Propane, 1-bromo- (1-Bromopropane) was also published for a 60-day public comment period ending on June 24, 2026. [2026-04-25]
- Information gathering : The updated Substance function codes and application codes (formerly known as Consumer and Commercial codes) were published. [2026-03-27]
- Calcium 2-ethylhexanoate and 2-ethylhexyl- 2-ethylhexanoate : A final order adding 2-ethylhexyl 2-ethylhexanoate to Part 2 of Schedule 1 of the Canadian Environmental Protection Act, 1999 was published. [2026-03-25]
- Significant New Activity orders and notices of the Chemicals Management Plan : An order amending the Domestic Substances List to vary the requirements under the Significant New Activity provisions of Canadian Environmental Protection Act, 1999 for 161 substances was published. [2026-03-25]
- Nanomaterials : The Framework for the Risk Assessment of Manufactured Nanomaterials under the Canadian Environmental Protection Act, 1999 was published. [2026-03-05]
- Order  amending Schedule 3 to Canadian Environmental Protection Act, 1999 : An order to amend Schedule 3 to  the Canadian Environmental Protection Act, 1999 (CEPA) was published. The  objective of the order is to establish export controls on six substances. [2026-02-25]
- Calcium  2-ethylhexanoate and 2-ethylhexyl-2-ethylhexanoate : An order amending the Domestic  Substances List to vary the requirements under the Significant New Activity  provisions to 2-ethylhexyl-2-ethylhexanoate  was published. [2026-02-25]
- Sodium  cyclamate and cyclohexylamine : An order amending the Domestic  Substances List to vary the requirements under the Significant New Activity  provisions to cyclohexanamine, also known as cyclohexylamine was published.  [2026-02-25]
- Benzophenone : The Proposed Code of Practice for Methanone, Diphenyl- (Benzophenone) in Paint, Stain and/or Coating Products Available to Consumers in Canada was published for a 60 day public comment period ending on April 15, 2026. [2026-02-14]
- Phenol, methylstyrenated : The Updated Draft Assessment for Phenol, methylstyrenated and the Revised Risk Management Scope for Phenol, methylstyrenated were published for a 60 day public comment period ending on March 18, 2026. [2026-01-17]
